events.text: Jennifer Young, 56-year-old Black female, Current smoker (0.5 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Malignant melanoma of the Left upper back. Clinical staging: T3bN2aM1c, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C43.9. Stage IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation, lung and brain metastases.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Underwent primary resection: Wide local excision with 2 cm margins and sentinel lymph node biopsy. Estimated blood loss 150 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy: Dabrafenib 150 mg BID + Trametinib 2 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for restaging on first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy.
Completed brain metastases treatment: Stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) 20 Gy single fraction to 2 brain metastases. Treatment delivered as planned.
Clinical visit for post-radiation assessment.
Initiated second-line immunotherapy: Nivolumab 480 mg q4w + Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg q3w x 4 then Nivolumab maintenance. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated second-line immunotherapy: Nivolumab 480 mg q4w + Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg q3w x 4 then Nivolumab maintenance. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated second-line immunotherapy: Nivolumab 480 mg q4w + Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg q3w x 4 then Nivolumab maintenance. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated second-line immunotherapy: Nivolumab 480 mg q4w + Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg q3w x 4 then Nivolumab maintenance. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 immune-related hepatitis. ALT/AST elevated to 5x ULN. Prednisone 1 mg/kg started. Immunotherapy held.
PET/CT performed for restaging after second-line immunotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after second-line immunotherapy.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Jennifer Young
MRN: patient_a54a6b7a62dd
Specimen ID: S2022-73214
Date of Procedure: 2022-07-17
Date of Report: 2022-07-20

SPECIMEN: Left upper back, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Malignant melanoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.1 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
S-100: Positive
HMB-45: Positive
Melan-A: Positive
SOX10: Positive
Ki-67: 30%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jennifer Young - Left upper back" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 6.1 x 4.3 x 3.0 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

BRAIN MRI WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Jennifer Young
MRN: patient_a54a6b7a62dd
Date of Study: 2022-07-18

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
BRAIN: 3 enhancing lesions identified: left occipital lobe (0.9 cm), left parietal lobe (1.9 cm), right cerebellum (1.3 cm). Surrounding vasogenic edema. No midline shift.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Dermatology

Patient: Jennifer Young
MRN: patient_a54a6b7a62dd
Date: 2022-07-24
Provider: Dr. Priya Sharma

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Young is a 56-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed malignant melanoma of the left upper back, IV. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation, lung and brain metastases. Pathology confirmed malignant melanoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports incidental finding on routine screening.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- hypertension
- osteoarthritis

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (0.5 ppd)
- Alcohol: Rare alcohol use
- Occupation: Nurse (retired)
- Lives with alone

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with prostate cancer at age 68. Paternal uncle with lung cancer.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- Lisinopril 10 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: dry mouth, intermittent nausea, dizziness, abdominal bloating. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 125/81, HR 70, RR 19, Temp 97.5F, SpO2 95% on RA, Wt 88.4 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.4, Hgb 12.2, Plt 245, Cr 1.14

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
56-year-old female with IV malignant melanoma of the left upper back. initial oncology consultation.

1. Malignant melanoma of the left upper back, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend primary resection
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Dermatology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Jennifer Young
MRN: patient_a54a6b7a62dd
Date of Report: 2022-07-30
Specimen: Left upper back (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Malignant melanoma
Panel: Tempus xT (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 11.8 mutations/Mb (Intermediate)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRAF p.V600E (c.1799T>A) - Missense (Exon 15) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TERT Promoter mutation (-124C>T) - Promoter
  CDKN2A Loss - Homozygous deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  NF1 p.T1556M - VUS
  ARID2 p.Q1334L - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRAF V600E: FDA-approved BRAF/MEK inhibitors include dabrafenib/trametinib, encorafenib/binimetinib, vemurafenib/cobimetinib.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 519x coverage.
Tumor content: 37%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Jennifer Young
MRN: patient_a54a6b7a62dd
Specimen ID: S2022-20805
Date of Procedure: 2022-08-17
Date of Report: 2022-08-21

SPECIMEN: Left upper back, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Malignant melanoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 2.1 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
S-100: Positive
HMB-45: Positive
Melan-A: Positive
SOX10: Positive
Ki-67: 30%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jennifer Young - Left upper back" is a firm tan tissue fragment measuring 2.1 x 1.5 x 1.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Sarah Chen, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

Date: 2022-08-25
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Young is a 56-year-old female with IV malignant melanoma of the left upper back who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient reports feeling well overall. Appetite has improved.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: insomnia, abdominal bloating, unintentional weight loss, joint pain. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 111/79, HR 88, RR 17, Temp 97.6F, SpO2 95% on RA, Wt 69.9 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.6, Hgb 14.5, Plt 104, Cr 1.03

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
56-year-old female with IV malignant melanoma of the left upper back. postoperative follow-up.

1. Malignant melanoma - postoperative follow-up
   - Restaging imaging to be scheduled
   - Will check labs prior to next cycle
2. Symptom management
   - Pain well controlled on current regimen
3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Jennifer Young
MRN: patient_a54a6b7a62dd
Date of Study: 2023-05-08

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. restaging after second-line immunotherapy.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 1.9 cm in the left upper lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Jennifer Young
MRN: patient_a54a6b7a62dd
Date of Study: 2023-07-27

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 2 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 0.6 cm in the left upper lobe. Stable in size.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: Lytic lesion in L2 vertebral body measuring 1.5 cm. Suspicious for metastasis.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.
3. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology
